explanations.1: The ${PROJECT_NAME:-your-project-name} syntax means 'use the PROJECT_NAME variable if it exists, otherwise use the default value your-project-name'.
explanations.10: The 'args' section passes build-time variables to the Docker build process, which is different from environment variables that are set when the container runs.
explanations.12: This is passing PHP_VERSION as a build argument so Docker knows which PHP version to install when creating the image.
explanations.23: The 'condition: service_healthy' means this container won't start until joomladb reports back that it's healthy (passing its healthcheck).
explanations.36: There's a typo here: it should be '${MYSQL_PORT:-3306}' not '${MYSQL_PORT-:3306}' — the colon is in the wrong place, which might cause this variable substitution to fail.
explanations.43: The healthcheck runs a MySQL ping command inside the container to verify the database is actually responding and ready to accept connections.
